headline: Amazon’s Globalstar Deal Recasts Satellite Ambitions And Investor Risk Reward
summary: Amazon.com, NasdaqGS:AMZN, has completed its acquisition of satellite operator Globalstar. This move expands Amazon’s direct to device satellite connectivity efforts and increases competitive pressure on other space based networks. headline: Apple withholds data in India antitrust case, watchdog sets final hearing
summary: Apple has not submitted data sought by India's antitrust body after an investigation found the U.S. firm abused its dominant position in the iPhone apps market, ‌prompting the watchdog to fast-track a decision on penalties to a final hearing next month, an order ‌shows.  The Competition Commission of India (CCI) said in an April 8 order that Apple has not submitted details of its financials and its views ​on the investigation since October 2024, and instead cited a separate case pending in the Delhi High Court where the company has challenged India's entire antitrust penalty law.  Apple, which denies any wrongdoing in the case, has said it fears it could be fined ‌up to $38 billion if the watchdog uses ⁠its global turnover to calculate penalties in the case.

content.title: Apple withholds data in India antitrust case, watchdog sets final hearing
content.summary: Apple has not submitted data sought by India's antitrust body after an investigation found the U.S. firm abused its dominant position in the iPhone apps market, ‌prompting the watchdog to fast-track a decision on penalties to a final hearing next month, an order ‌shows.  The Competition Commission of India (CCI) said in an April 8 order that Apple has not submitted details of its financials and its views ​on the investigation since October 2024, and instead cited a separate case pending in the Delhi High Court where the company has challenged India's entire antitrust penalty law.  Apple, which denies any wrongdoing in the case, has said it fears it could be fined ‌up to $38 billion if the watchdog uses ⁠its global turnover to calculate penalties in the case.

headline: Why the Dow Is Beating the S&P 500 Today
summary: Salesforce and a handful of other larger stocks were keeping the Dow around breakeven on Monday.  Only 13 of the Dow's 30 stocks were rising, but the blue-chip index was moving in and out of positive territory in the first-half or so of trading.  Other big stocks lifting the Dow were International Business Machines, JPMorgan Chase, Apple, and Travelers, which together added nearly 100 points.

content.title: Why the Dow Is Beating the S&P 500 Today
content.summary: Salesforce and a handful of other larger stocks were keeping the Dow around breakeven on Monday.  Only 13 of the Dow's 30 stocks were rising, but the blue-chip index was moving in and out of positive territory in the first-half or so of trading.  Other big stocks lifting the Dow were International Business Machines, JPMorgan Chase, Apple, and Travelers, which together added nearly 100 points.

headline: Anthropic's CEO goes to the White House to defuse a feud with the Pentagon
summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is set to meet the White House chief of staff to address the company’s dispute with the U.S. government. The Pentagon labeled Anthropic a “supply-chain risk,” prompting a lawsuit and mixed court rulings, including one blocking restrictions on Claude AI and another allowing blacklisting to continue during litigation. The conflict followed Anthropic’s refusal to allow its models for autonomous weapons or domestic surveillance. Despite the standoff, the company reports rapid growth, from about $9 billion in annualized revenue at the end of 2025 to over $30 billion, more than 1,000 million-dollar clients, and a $100 million Project Glasswing with Apple and Microsoft.

headline: Factbox-Who is John Ternus, Apple's new CEO?
summary: Apple on Monday named insider John Ternus as its next CEO, tasking the longtime hardware chief with steering ‌the company after Tim Cook as the iPhone maker prepares ‌for industrywide shifts driven by artificial intelligence. The leadership change marks the end of an era ​at one of the world's most iconic companies, just as rivals bet on products that use AI to loosen Apple's grip on the consumer electronics market. Here are some key facts about Ternus: LONGTIME VETERAN Ternus joined Apple's product design ‌team in 2001 and ⁠became a vice president of hardware engineering in 2013.

headline: Apple CEO Cook is lauded by investors as he prepares for new role
summary: Apple shares declined less than 1% in late trading on Monday after the communications hardware firm said its chief executive, Tim Cook, would step down after nearly 15 years at the helm of the world's second most-valuable company.  The decision by Cook, ‌65 years old, to step aside in favor of longtime Apple hardware chief John Ternus took Wall Street by surprise and will raise questions about ‌whether the new chief can maintain the brisk pace set by his predecessor.  Cook will become executive chairman on September 1 as the iPhone maker gears up for industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

content.title: Apple CEO Cook is lauded by investors as he prepares for new role
content.summary: Apple shares declined less than 1% in late trading on Monday after the communications hardware firm said its chief executive, Tim Cook, would step down after nearly 15 years at the helm of the world's second most-valuable company.  The decision by Cook, ‌65 years old, to step aside in favor of longtime Apple hardware chief John Ternus took Wall Street by surprise and will raise questions about ‌whether the new chief can maintain the brisk pace set by his predecessor.  Cook will become executive chairman on September 1 as the iPhone maker gears up for industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

headline: Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO and hand reins over to the iPhone maker's hardware leader
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down from the job that he inherited from the late Steve Jobs, ending a nearly 15-year reign that saw the company’s market value soar by more than $3.6 trillion during an iPhone-fueled era of prosperity.  Cook, 65, will turn the CEO duties over to Apple’s head of hardware engineering, John Ternus, on Sept. 1 while remaining involved with the Cupertino, California, company as executive chairman.  “It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company," Cook said in a statement.

content.title: Tim Cook will step down as Apple CEO and hand reins over to the iPhone maker's hardware leader
content.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down from the job that he inherited from the late Steve Jobs, ending a nearly 15-year reign that saw the company’s market value soar by more than $3.6 trillion during an iPhone-fueled era of prosperity.  Cook, 65, will turn the CEO duties over to Apple’s head of hardware engineering, John Ternus, on Sept. 1 while remaining involved with the Cupertino, California, company as executive chairman.  “It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company," Cook said in a statement.

content.title: John Ternus to become the next CEO of Apple
content.description: Yahoo Finance tech editor Dan Howley discusses John Ternus, who is set to become Apple's next CEO on September 1, 2026, positioning him to lead the highly anticipated launch of the company's first foldable iPhone just weeks after taking the helm.
content.summary: Yahoo Finance tech editor Dan Howley discusses John Ternus, who is set to become Apple's next CEO on September 1, 2026, positioning him to lead the highly anticipated launch of the company's first foldable iPhone just weeks after taking the helm.

headline: Tim Cook Leaving Apple in 'Good Shape,' Gil Luria Says
summary: "Apple is in good shape," Gil Luria, D.A. Davidson head of technology research, says while reacting to Apple naming John Ternus as the iPhone maker's next leader, with Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close."

headline: Apple says John Ternus will be new CEO, Tim Cook will transition to executive chairman
summary: Apple on Monday named John Ternus as its new CEO, effective Sept. 1. Ternus will take over from Tim Cook, who has led Apple for 15 years.

content.title: Apple says John Ternus will be new CEO, Tim Cook will transition to executive chairman
content.summary: Apple on Monday named John Ternus as its new CEO, effective Sept. 1. Ternus will take over from Tim Cook, who has led Apple for 15 years.

thumbnail.caption: John Ternus, senior vice president of hardware engineering at Apple Inc., during an Apple event in New York, on March 4, 2026.

headline: Apple Has Unusual Put Options Activity - Has AAPL Stock Peaked Ahead of Earnings?
summary: Apple stock is showing unusually heavy put options activity ahead of its earnings release at the end of April. AAPL has been rising, so investors may think it has peaked. However, analysts have higher price targets.

content.title: Apple Has Unusual Put Options Activity - Has AAPL Stock Peaked Ahead of Earnings?
content.summary: Apple stock is showing unusually heavy put options activity ahead of its earnings release at the end of April. AAPL has been rising, so investors may think it has peaked. However, analysts have higher price targets.

headline: Alphabet at $4 Trillion: Why the King of Tech Is Feeling Cramped on Planet Earth
summary: Alphabet has successfully defended its dominance, effectively becoming a proxy for the global economy. While its current valuation is sound, its huge scale now creates a growth ceiling tethered to global GDP, forcing a dilemma where future outperformance depends on inventing entirely new market paradigms.

content.title: Alphabet at $4 Trillion: Why the King of Tech Is Feeling Cramped on Planet Earth
content.summary: Alphabet has successfully defended its dominance, effectively becoming a proxy for the global economy. While its current valuation is sound, its huge scale now creates a growth ceiling tethered to global GDP, forcing a dilemma where future outperformance depends on inventing entirely new market paradigms.
